common_ai_mistakes.name: include_everything
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI often includes unnecessary headers. Only include what you use. Use forward declarations to reduce compile-time dependencies.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect large numbers of includes that may be unnecessary.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Remove unused includes. Use forward declarations in headers. Include what you use, nothing more.

common_ai_mistakes.name: unnecessary_this
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI often uses this-> in C++ where it's not needed. Only use this-> to disambiguate from local variables.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect this-> usage where member name doesn't shadow a local.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Remove unnecessary this-> prefix. Use it only when a parameter shadows a member name.

common_ai_mistakes.name: iostream_for_logging
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI defaults to std::cout for logging. Production code should use a proper logging library with levels, timestamps, and output control.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect std::cout usage for logging/debugging in non-trivial code.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Use spdlog, glog, or a custom logging wrapper with severity levels.

common_ai_mistakes.name: string_by_value_return
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI sometimes worries about returning std::string by value. Modern C++ has RVO/NRVO — returning by value is fine and idiomatic.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect code that avoids returning string by value (using out params instead).
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Return std::string by value. The compiler applies RVO/NRVO to eliminate the copy.
